name: Unbounded Consumption
what_it_means: Without resource limits, attackers can exhaust LLM computational resources, drive up costs, trigger denial-of-service conditions, or cause cascading failures through excessive token consumption or API abuse.
animated_example: Like a restaurant with no menu prices and unlimited refills — one table orders everything on the menu all night, and the kitchen collapses.
quick_checks: Are per-user token limits and rate limits enforced at the API and application layer?
Are abnormally large prompts or context windows rejected before reaching the LLM?
Is cost and usage monitoring in place with alerts for spike anomalies?
Are recursive or chain-of-thought prompts that could cause runaway token generation detected and capped?

payload_types.description: Submitting extremely large inputs to maximize token consumption and cost
examples: Repeating a single word 50,000 times in a prompt
Submitting a maximally-padded prompt on every request
mitigation: Enforce strict maximum input token limits per request
Reject requests exceeding defined size thresholds before LLM processing

payload_types.description: Crafting prompts that cause the LLM to generate very long outputs
examples: 'List every country, every city, every street...'
mitigation: Apply output length monitoring and circuit breakers

payload_types.description: Prompts that cause the LLM or agent to loop indefinitely
examples: Agent instructed to process its own output repeatedly
Prompt: 'Expand the previous answer in full detail, then expand again'
Multi-agent feedback loop without termination condition
mitigation: Implement loop detection and maximum iteration counts in agentic systems
Set absolute wall-clock and token budgets for all agent runs

payload_types.description: Systematically querying the LLM API to extract model knowledge or capabilities
examples: Automated queries to map model knowledge boundaries
High-volume query campaigns to extract training data
Systematic probing of model behavior for competitive intelligence
mitigation: Apply rate limiting and CAPTCHA/bot detection on high-frequency query patterns
Monitor for systematic probing behavior and block offending accounts

how_to_test: Submit oversized inputs (megabyte-scale) and verify they are rejected with an error before LLM processing
Test output amplification: request extremely long outputs and verify the response is capped
Simulate high-frequency API calls from a single user to verify rate limiting kicks in
Test recursive agent workflows for loop detection and termination
Verify cost alerts fire when usage spikes beyond expected thresholds
expected_outcomes: Oversized inputs are rejected at the API gateway before reaching the LLM
Output length is capped; runaway generation is terminated
Rate limits trigger and block abusive usage patterns
minimal_example: User sends 500KB prompt → API gateway rejects at input validation (413 Payload Too Large) → LLM never receives request
mitigation: Enforce hard input token limits per request at the API gateway layer — reject oversized prompts before they reach the LLM, returning a 413 error with clear limits documented for legitimate users
Implement per-user rate limiting with graduated throttling (warn → slow → block) and apply output token caps to prevent runaway generation from amplification prompts or recursive agent loops

Deploy real-time cost and usage monitoring with automated alerts for anomalous consumption spikes — set budget alerts to trigger before costs reach unacceptable thresholds, with automatic circuit-breaker responses
Implement loop detection and maximum iteration/token budgets for all agentic and multi-step workflows — terminate any agent run that exceeds its defined resource envelope and log the event for review
